stem: What does the Baudhayan theorem (Baudhayana Sulva Sutras) relate to?
options: Lengths of sides of a right-angled triangle
Calculation of the value of pi

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Lengths of sides of a right-angled triangle
solution.detail: The Baudhayan Sulva Sutras contain one of the earliest known statements of the Pythagorean theorem, relating to the lengths of the sides of a right-angled triangle. This ancient Indian text provided a geometric construction method for determining the length of the hypotenuse based on the other sides of the rightangled triangle. It predates the work of Pythagoras. The theorem is significant in the history of Indian mathematics and geometry.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Who among the following scientists shared the Nobel Prize in Physics with his son?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: William Henry Bragg
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: Max Planck won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1918 for his discovery of quantum theory, but he did not share it with his son. Option (b) is incorrect: Einstein was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his work on the photoelectric effect, but he did not share it with his son. Option (c) is correct: William Henry Bragg along with his son, William Lawrence Bragg, received the Nobel Prize in 1915 for their X-ray crystallography research, which helped in determining crystal structures. Option (d) is incorrect: Enrico Fermi won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1938 for his work on nuclear reactions, but he did not share it with his son.

optionNotes.text: Max Planck won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1918 for his discovery of quantum theory, but he did not share it with his son.
Einstein was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his work on the photoelectric effect, but he did not share it with his son.
William Henry Bragg along with his son, William Lawrence Bragg, received the Nobel Prize in 1915 for their X-ray crystallography research, which helped in determining crystal structures.
Enrico Fermi won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1938 for his work on nuclear reactions, but he did not share it with his son.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Ogaden region has been a source of conflict between which countries?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Ethiopia and Somalia
solution.detail: The Ogaden region has been a long-standing disputed territory between Ethiopia and Somalia. In 1977–1978, Somalia invaded Ethiopia in the Ogaden War, attempting to annex the region, but was eventually pushed back with Soviet and Cuban assistance to Ethiopia. Even today, the Ogaden conflict remains a source of tension due to the Ogaden National Liberation Front (ONLF), a separatist group fighting for independence from Ethiopia.

subject: Current Affairs

subject: Current Affairs

subject: Current Affairs
stem: How is the United Nations Monetary and Financial Conference where the agreements were signed to set up the IBRD, GATT and IMF, commonly known?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Bretton Woods Conference
solution.detail: The United Nations Monetary and Financial Conference (1944), commonly known as Bretton Woods conference, was held in Bretton Woods, New Hampshire, USA to regulate the international monetary and financial order after the conclusion of World War II. During this conference, agreements were signed to establish the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD), the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT), and the International Monetary Fund (IMF).

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Near which one of the following cities are Palitana Temples located?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Bhavnagar
solution.detail:
Palitana Temples is a well-known Jain pilgrimage place in Gujarat that is close to Bhavnagar. They are composed of more than 800 finely carved marble temples dedicated to 24 Tirthankars. and are located atop Shatrunjaya Hill. Every pious Jain is obliged to make the Palitana Yatra (pilgrimage) at least once in their lifetime because the temples are regarded as one of the holiest places in Jainism. Many Jain patrons contributed to the construction of these temples over a period of 900 years. Famous Temples in Mount Abu (Rajasthan)
- Dilwara Temples – A group of exquisitely carved Jain temples built between the 11th and 13th centuries, known for their intricate marble craftsmanship.
- Adhar Devi Temple – Dedicated to Goddess Durga, built inside a natural cave, requiring 365 steps to reach.
- Raghunath Temple – A Vaishnavite temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu, known for its historical and spiritual significance. Famous Temples in Nasik (Maharashtra)
- Trimbakeshwar Temple – One of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva, located near the origin of the Godavari River.
- Kalaram Temple – An ancient temple dedicated to Lord Rama, built entirely of black stone.
- Sundarnarayan Temple – A Vishnu temple, famous for its unique alignment with the rising sun during the equinox.
- Kapaleshwar Temple – A rare Shiva temple without a Nandi idol, as it is believed that Shiva himself performed penance here. Famous Temples in Ujjain (Madhya Pradesh):
- Mahakaleshwar Temple – One of the 12 Jyotirlingas, known for its Bhasma Aarti, where sacred ash is offered to Lord Shiva every morning.
- Kal Bhairav Temple – Dedicated to Lord Bhairav, where liquor is offered as a ritual to the deity.
- Harsiddhi Temple – One of the Shakti Peethas, dedicated to Goddess Durga.
- Chintaman Ganesh Temple – An ancient temple where Lord Ganesha is worshipped as the “remover of worries”.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: With which one of the following is BRIT (Gover- nment of India) engaged?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Isotope Technology
solution.detail:
BRIT (Board of Radiation & Isotope Technology) is an organisation under the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), Government of India.
- It specialises in isotope applications, radiopharmaceuticals, industrial radiography, and radiation processing.
- BRIT provides radioisotopes and radiation technologies for a range of uses, which are essential to nuclear medicine, agriculture, industry, and research.
- It supports healthcare by providing radioisotopes for cancer diagnosis and treatment and contributes to food preservation, sterilisation of medical products, and industrial testing.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Rajiv Gandhi National Flying Institute is being established in which state?

solution.detail: The Rajiv Gandhi National Flying Institute (RGNFI) is being established in Gondia, Maharashtra. The institute aims to enhance aviation training and produce highly skilled commercial pilots to meet the growing demands of the aviation sector. It is equipped with modern training aircraft, simulators, and world-class infrastructure.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: What is the number of spokes in the Dharmachakra in the National Flag of India?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 24
solution.detail: The Dharmachakra (Wheel of Dharma) in the center of the Indian National Flag is known as the Ashoka Chakra. It has 24 spokes, symbolizing righteousness, progress, and perpetual motion. The Ashoka Chakra is derived from the Lion Capital of Ashoka at Sarnath, which is India’s national emblem. The 24 spokes represent the 24 virtues that guide life, such as truth, justice, compassion, and perseverance.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which was the Capital of Andhra State when it was made a separate State in the year 1953?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Kurnool
solution.detail: The first linguistic state in India, Andhra State, was established on October 1, 1953, after being separated from the Madras Presidency. From 1953 until 1956, Kurnool served as the capital of Andhra State. Hyderabad became the capital after Andhra Pradesh was formed in 1956 (after Telangana and Hyderabad State merged).

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which one of the following is the largest (area- wise) Lok Sabha constituency?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Ladakh
solution.detail: Ladakh Lok Sabha constit-uency is the largest parliamentary constituency in India by area. It covers the entire Ladakh region of Jammu & Kashmir (now a Union Territory since 2019), spanning over 173,266 square kilometers. Despite being the largest in area, it has one of the lowest populations among Lok Sabha constituencies due to its harsh terrain and sparse settlements.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: How can the height of a person who is six feet tall be expressed (approximately) in nanometers?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 183 × 107 nanometres
solution.detail:
Convert feet to centimetres:
- 1 foot = 30.48 cm
- 6 feet = 6 × 30.48 = 182.88 cm Convert centimeters to nanometers:
- 1 cm = 10⁷ nanometers

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Ebraham Alkazi is an eminent personality in which one of the following areas?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Theatre training
solution.detail: In India, Ebrahim Alkazi was a well-known theatre director, acting instructor, and art enthusiast. From 1962 until 1977, he was the Director of the National School of Drama (NSD), where he transformed Indian theatre education. By fusing Indian customs with Western methods, Alkazi significantly contributed to the modernisation of Indian theatre. He also served as a tutor to Naseeruddin Shah and Om Puri, among other well-known performers. For his services to Indian theatre, he received the Padma Shri, Padma Bhushan, and Padma Vibhushan.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Bimbavati Devi is a well-known dancer of which type of dance?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Manipuri
solution.detail: Bimbavati Devi is a renowned Manipuri classical dancer. She is the daughter of Kalavati Devi and Guru Bipin Singh, two renowned Manipuri dancers. She has promoted Raas Leela and other traditional Manipuri dance styles through her numerous performances in India and overseas. The traditional Vaishnavite ceremonial motions of ancient Manipuri form and delicate developments for her modern productions are expertly balanced in Bimbavati Devi’s present style.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: How is Sarosh Zaiwalla well-known as?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: A leading international legal expert
solution.detail: Sarosh Zaiwalla is a renowned international legal expert and the founder of Zaiwalla & Co., a London-based law firm. He handled well-known international arbitration and litigation issues and was the first lawyer of Indian descent to open a practice in the United Kingdom. Russian corporations, Middle Eastern organisations, and the Indian government are just a few of the world leaders he has represented. His contributions to arbitration and international law are well known.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Elizabeth Hawley is well-known for her writings relating to which one of the following?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Himalayan expeditions
solution.detail: Elizabeth Hawley (1923–2018) was an American journalist and historian renowned for documenting Himalayan expeditions, particularly mountaineering in Nepal. She meticulously recorded details of Mount Everest and other Himalayan climbs, making her one of the most trusted sources on Himalayan mountaineering history. She maintained a comprehensive database of Himalayan expeditions for over five decades, authoring The Himalayan Database, a key reference for mountaineers. Though never climbing herself, her records were considered authoritative.

stem: Consider the following statements with reference to Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS): 1. All persons of 60 years or above belonging to the households below poverty line in rural areas are eligible. 2. The Central Assistance under this Scheme is at the rate of ` 300 per month per beneficiary. Under the Scheme, States have been urged to give matching amounts. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Neither 1 nor 2
solution.detail: The Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS) is a part of the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) launched by the Government of India to support elderly individuals from below-poverty-line (BPL) households. Statement 1 is incorrect: The scheme is applicable to individuals aged 60 years or above who belong to BPL households. However the eligibility is not restricted to rural areas alone as it encompasses both rural and urban BPL households. subject: Environment
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Salt-water crocodile is found in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. 2. Shrew and tapir are found in the Western Ghats of the Malabar region. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The salt-water crocodile, the largest living reptile, is found in coastal regions of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. These islands provide a suitable habitat for the species, including mangrove swamps and estuarine areas, which are ideal for the crocodile’s nesting and hunting. Statement 2 is correct: The shrew is a small mammal found in the forested areas of the Western Ghats, including the Malabar region. The most common is Malabar Shrew. The tapir, specifically the Malayan tapir, is found in the rainforests of the Western Ghats. Tapirs are herbivorous mammals that are adapted to the dense forests and are native to parts of South and Southeast Asia, including the Western Ghats.

subject: Environment
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) in respect of carbon credits is one of the Kyoto Protocol Mechanisms. 2. Under the CDM, the projects handled pertain only to the Annex-I countries. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: The Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) under the UNFCCC enables developing countries to receive funds from developed nations for adopting technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions. It allows developed countries to invest in emission-reduction projects in developing countries. In return, these developed countries earn carbon credits while promoting sustainable development. Statement 1 is correct. CDM is indeed one of the Kyoto Protocol mechanisms designed to help reduce greenhouse gas emissions by allowing developed countries to invest in emission-reduction projects in developing countries. Statement 2 is incorrect. CDM projects are implemented in developing countries, not in Annex I countries (which are mostly developed nations). The idea is that developed countries (Annex I) can earn carbon credits by funding emission-reducing projects in developing countries (non-Annex I). 6. ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS, CONVENTIONS AND POLICIES

subject: Environment
stem: Where was the World Summit on Sustainable Development (Rio + 10) held?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Johannesburg
solution.detail: The World Summit on Sustainable Development (WSSD), also known as Rio+10, was held in Johannesburg, South Africa, in 2002. This summit marked the 10th anniversary of the Earth Summit held in Rio de Janeiro, in 1992. The WSSD focused on sustainable development issues, including poverty alleviation, environmental conservation, and the promotion of sustainable practices worldwide. In 1992, the Earth Summit (United Nations Conference on Environment and Development) was held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. It resulted in key outcomes such as the Rio Declaration, Agenda 21, and the establishment of the Convention on Biological Diversity and the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC).

subject: Geography
topic: Solar System And Evolution Of Earth

stem: In order of their distances from the Sun, which of the following planets lie between Mars and Uranus?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Jupiter and Saturn
solution.detail: Order of Planets in the Solar System (from the Sun) is Mercury> Venus > Earth > Mars > Jupiter > Saturn > Uranus > Neptune. Mars is the 4th planet from the Sun and Uranus is the 7th planet from the Sun. Therefore the planets that lie between Mars (4th) and Uranus (7th) are Jupiter (5th) and Saturn (6th).

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following statements : 1. The albedo of an object determines its visual brightness when viewed with reflected light. 2. The albedo of Mercury is much greater than the albedo of the Earth. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: Albedo is the measure of a surface’s reflectivity, with higher albedo indicating greater reflection of sunlight, influencing Earth’s temperature and climate. Statement 1 is correct: Albedo refers to the reflectivity of a surface, or the fraction of light that a surface reflects. A higher albedo means more reflection of light, which causes an object to appear brighter when viewed with reflected light. This is why snowy surfaces (with high albedo) appear brighter than forests or oceans (with low albedo). Statement 2 is incorrect: Mercury actually has a much lower albedo than Earth. Mercury’s rocky and heavily cratered surface reflects only about 12 to 14% of sunlight, meaning it absorbs the vast majority of incoming sunlight and appears relatively dark. On the other hand, Earth has a higher albedo due to its cloud cover, oceans, and ice caps, which reflect a significant portion of sunlight back into space. Earth’s average albedo is around 0.30 to 0.35.

subject: Geography
stem: For India, China, UK and USA, which one of the following is the correct sequence of the median age of their population?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: India < China USA < UK
solution.detail:
Median age refers to the age at which half the population is younger and half is older. It reflects the demographic profile of a country, with higher median ages indicating older populations. With respect to year in which question has been asked:
- India: India, being a developing nation with a younger population and a higher birth rate, has the lowest median age among these countries.
- China: China’s population control policies (like the onechild policy) and the country’s demographic transition have resulted in a population that is older than India’s but younger than that of the USA and the UK.
- USA: The USA has a relatively older population compared to India and China due to factors such as higher life expectancy and a stable birth rate. Therefore, the median age is higher than those of India and China but lower than the UK’s.
- UK: The UK, being an industrialized and developed nation with an aging population, has the highest median age among the four countries. The population has a stable growth rate and a higher proportion of elderly individuals, making the median age of the UK the highest.

subject: Geography
stem: As per India’s National Population Policy, 2000, by which one of the following years is it our long-term objective to achieve population stabilization?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2045
solution.detail: India’s National Population Policy, 2000, sets a longterm objective to achieve a stable population by the year 2045. This goal aligns with sustainable economic growth, social development, and environmental protection. The policy emphasizes addressing unmet needs for contraception, healthcare infrastructure, and personnel, aiming to bring the Total Fertility Rate (TFR) to replacement levels by 2010. The medium-term objective is to achieve a TFR of 2.1 by 2010, and the long-term objective is to stabilize the population by 2045.

subject: Geography
stem: What is the approximate percentage of persons above 65 years of age in India’s current population?

subject: Geography
stem: Which one amongst the following has the largest livestock population in the world ?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: India
solution.detail:
Option (c) is correct: India holds the largest livestock population in the world, with about 192.5 million cattle and a total bovine population of 307.5 million. India holds around 14-15% of the world’s cattle and leads globally in buffalo population. Options (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect:
- Brazil has the second-largest cattle population globally, with approximately 194.4 million cattle. However, while Brazil excels in beef production and exports, its total livestock population (including cattle, poultry, goats, etc.) is less than India’s.
- China has a large livestock population, particularly in poultry and pigs. It is the world’s largest producer of pork and has a significant poultry industry. However, when considering total livestock (including cattle, buffaloes, goats, etc.), China still lags behind India.
- The United States has a significant cattle population (~94 million), focusing on beef production and exports. The USA also leads in poultry and pig farming, but its total livestock population is lower than India’s.

subject: Geography
stem: With which one of the following rivers is the Omkareshwar Project associated?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Narmada
solution.detail:
The Omkareshwar Project, a multi-purpose dam project, is located in the Khandwa district of Madhya Pradesh, India. This project utilizes the Narmada River, a major river system in central India, to achieve several key objectives such as:
- A primary function is the generation of hydroelectric power, with a station capacity of 520 MW, contributing substantially to the regional energy supply.
- The project is intended to provide irrigation to extensive agricultural lands, thereby increasing agricultural productivity and supporting local economies.
- The Omkareshwar Project also plays a role in supplying water for domestic and industrial use, ensuring access to this vital resource. The dam itself is situated on the Narmada River, proximate to the town of Omkareshwar, a significant Hindu pilgrimage site. The town’s religious importance derives from the presence of the Omkareshwar Temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva.

subject: Geography
stem: In India, how many states share the coastline?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 9
solution.detail:
India’s extensive coastline, stretching approximately 7,516 kilometers, is shared by nine diverse states, each contributing unique characteristics to the nation’s maritime heritage.
- Starting from the west: Gujarat boasts the longest coastline in India which dotted with numerous ports and playing a crucial role in maritime trade. Maharashtra’s coastline is home to the bustling metropolis of Mumbai, a major commercial and entertainment hub. Goa is renowned for its beautiful beaches and vibrant tourism industry. Karnataka’s coastline is significant for its port cities and coastal cuisine. Kerala is famed for its backwaters, lush greenery, and historical spice trade connections.
- On the eastern coast: Tamil Nadu possesses a long coastline with important ports and cultural significance, including the historic city of Chennai. Andhra Pradesh which is located north of Tamil Nadu has a substantial coastline crucial for fishing and maritime activities. Odisha, known for its rich cultural heritage, also has a significant coastline vulnerable to cyclones. West Bengal features a coastline that includes the Sundarbans, a unique mangrove ecosystem and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. In addition to the nine states, India’s coastline is also shared by four Union Territories: Andaman and Nicobar Islands archipelago in the Bay of Bengal boasts the longest coastline among all the Union Territories. These islands are known for their pristine beaches, diverse marine life, and lush tropical forests. They hold strategic importance for India and are a hub for tourism and biodiversity. Lakshadweep located in the Arabian Sea is a group of coral islands. These islands are known for their beautiful lagoons, coral reefs, and white sandy beaches. They are a popular destination for those seeking tranquility and water sports activities. Puducherry on the southeastern coast of India has a relatively short coastline. Puducherry is known for its French colonial heritage, serene atmosphere, and the Sri Aurobindo Ashram. Its coastline features beaches and promenades. Daman and Diu is situated on the western coast, Daman and Diu is a Union Territory with a coastline along the Arabian Sea. Daman is on the mainland, while Diu is a small island. They are known for their beaches, historical sites, and relaxed atmosphere.

subject: Geography
stem: Which of the following hills are found where the Eastern Ghats and the Western Ghats meet?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Nilgiri Hills
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: Anamalai Hills, also known as the Elephant Hills, are a mountain range located in the Western Ghats, primarily within the states of Kerala and Tamil Nadu. They are renowned for their high peaks, including Anamudi, the highest peak in Southern India. While significant within the Western Ghats, the Anamalai Hills are not situated at the confluence of the Eastern and Western Ghats. Option (b) is incorrect: The Cardamom Hills, part of the Western Ghats, are situated in Kerala and Tamil Nadu. They are named for the cardamom spice that is cultivated extensively in this region. These hills are known for their biodiversity and dense forests. Option (c) is correct: The Nilgiri Hills are a mountain range located in the Western Ghats of Tamil Nadu. They are recognized as the junction point of the Eastern and Western Ghats mountain ranges. The region is characterized by its rolling hills, tea plantations, and diverse flora and fauna. The Nilgiris’ significance lies in their unique geographical position which provides bridge between the two distinct mountain systems. Ooty (Udhagamandalam), the famous hill station is located in the Nilgiris. Option (d) is incorrect: The Shevaroy Hills are a smaller mountain range located in the Eastern Ghats of Tamil Nadu. They are known for their coffee plantations and the hill station of Yercaud. As part of the Eastern Ghats, the Shevaroy Hills are geographically separate from the meeting point of the Eastern and Western Ghats, which is defined by the Nilgiri Hills.

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following pairs: Tributary River Main River 1. Chambal Narmada 2. Sone Yamuna 3. Manas Brahmaputra Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 3 only
solution.detail: Pair (1) is incorrectly matched: The Chambal River is a major tributary of the Yamuna River, a significant river in North India. The Narmada River, on the other hand, flows westward and is independent of the Yamuna and Chambal river systems. These two rivers are geographically separated and do not share a tributary-main river relationship. Pair (2) is incorrectly matched: The Sone River is a tributary of the Ganga River, not the Yamuna. It originates in the Amarkantak Plateau (Madhya Pradesh) and joins the Ganga near Patna. Pair (3) is correctly matched: Manas originates in the Himalayas in Bhutan and flows through Assam. It joins the Brahmaputra near Jogighopa. It is known for its rich biodiversity and is part of the Manas National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

subject: Geography
stem: In which one of the following states are Namchik Namphuk coalfields located?

solution.detail:
Coal mining plays a crucial role in India’s energy sector, and the Namchik-Namphuk coalfields are an important part of India’s northeastern coal reserves. Option (a) is correct: Arunachal Pradesh: Arunachal Pradesh’s primary coal resource is concentrated in the Namchik-Namphuk coalfields located in the Changlang district. These are the most significant coal-bearing areas in the state, though they are relatively small compared to major coalfields elsewhere in India. The coal found here is generally of tertiary age. Option (b), (c) and (d) are incorrect:
- Meghalaya possesses coal deposits, primarily in the Garo Hills region, which includes areas like Nangalbibra, Siju, and others. The coal here is typically sub-bituminous. It’s important to note that much of the coal mining in Meghalaya is done through small-scale, often unregulated operations (rat-hole mining) which has raised environmental concerns.
- Manipur has limited coal resources, and no significant, named coalfields are widely recognized. Coal occurrences are scattered and generally not considered commercially viable. Exploration has been carried out in areas like the Ukhrul district, but large-scale mining operations are not a feature of the state.
- Mizoram has no significant coalfields within the state. Coal occurrences are scattered and not considered economically significant. The geological formations in Mizoram are not conducive to the formation of large, commercially viable coal deposits.

subject: Geography
stem: Where are Tapovan and Vishnugarh hydroelectric project located?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Uttarakhand
solution.detail: The Tapovan and Vishnugarh hydroelectric projects are located in Chamoli district of Uttarakhand. These projects utilize the waters of the Dhauliganga River. The Tapovan-Vishnugarh project is a run-of-river scheme, meaning it harnesses the natural flow of the river without creating a large storage reservoir. Developed by NTPC Limited, a major power generation company in India, the Tapovan-Vishnugarh project has a planned installed capacity of 520 MW. This capacity represents a substantial contribution to the power generation capabilities of Uttarakhand and the nation. Run-of-river projects like this generally have a smaller environmental footprint compared to large dam projects with vast reservoirs.

subject: Geography
stem: In which one of the following is Malta located?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Mediterranean Sea
solution.detail:
Malta is an island country situated in the central Mediterranean Sea. It is strategically located between Sicily (Italy) to the north and North Africa (specifically Tunisia and Libya) to the south. Option (a), (c) and (d) are incorrect:
- The Baltic Sea is located in Northern Europe bordered by countries such as Sweden, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, and Germany. It is a brackish inland sea and it plays a significant role in regional trade and ecology in Northern Europe.
- The Black Sea lies to the northeast of Malta, bordered by Turkey, Bulgaria, Romania, Ukraine, and Russia. It is surrounded by countries in Eastern Europe and Western Asia and is important for trade routes, especially for countries like Russia and Ukraine.
- The North Sea is located in Northern Europe, bordered by Norway, Denmark, Germany, the Netherlands, Belgium, and the United Kingdom. It is a cold, productive sea that serves as a major fishing area and an important oil-producing region.

subject: Geography
stem: Which of the following cities is nearest to the equator?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Singapore
solution.detail:
The equator is an imaginary line that divides the Earth into two hemispheres: the Northern Hemisphere and the Southern Hemisphere. Singapore is located just north of the equator, at 1° 17′ N, making it the closest city to the equator in this list. It is a global financial hub and one of the world’s busiest ports. Its proximity to the equator means that it experiences a tropical rainforest climate characterized by high humidity, heavy rainfall, and consistent temperatures year-round. Option (a), (b) and (c) are incorrect:
- Colombo is situated to the north of the equator, about 7 degrees away. The tropical climate in Colombo means it experiences a warm, humid environment year-round. It is located on the west coast of Sri Lanka and serves as its commercial capital.
- Jakarta is located at approximately at 6° 9′ S latitude and 106° 49′ E longitude. It lies just south of the equator, about 6 degrees away. It experiences a warm tropical rainforest climate, with consistent rainfall and temperatures throughout the year. Jakarta is the capital city of Indonesia and one of the largest urban centers in Southeast Asia.
- Manila is situated at 14 degrees north of the equator. While it is still within the tropical zone, it is farther from the equator compared to Colombo, Jakarta, and Singapore. It is the capital of the Philippines and is located on the western side of Luzon Island which is the largest island in the Philippines.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following straits is nearest to the International Date Line?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Bering Strait
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: The Malacca Strait lies between the Malay Peninsula (in Malaysia) and the Sumatra Island (in Indonesia). It connects the Andaman Sea to the South China Sea and is a crucial shipping route. The Malacca Strait is located around 100° E longitude, which is far to the east of the International Date Line (at 180° longitude). It is relatively far from the IDL compared to the other options. Option (b) is correct: The Bering Strait is extremely close to the International Date Line. The Bering Strait is located between the Easternmost point of Russia (Chukchi Peninsula) and the Alaskan coast of the United States. It connects the Arctic Ocean with the Bering Sea. The IDL passes through the Bering Strait, making it the closest to the IDL of all the options listed. This proximity means that the time zone on one side of the strait can be almost a full 24 hours ahead or behind the time zone on the other side, depending on the direction of travel. Option (c) is incorrect: The Strait of Florida lies between Florida (USA) and the Bahamian Islands, connecting the Gulf of Mexico with the Atlantic Ocean. It is located at around 81° W longitude, far to the west of the International Date Line, which places it quite far from the IDL. Option (d) is incorrect: The Strait of Gibraltar is located between the southern tip of Spain and the northern tip of Morocco, connecting the Atlantic Ocean with the Mediterranean Sea. It is located at about 5° W longitude, much farther west of the International Date Line.

stem: During the time of which Mughal Emperor did the English East India Company establish its first factory in India?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Jahangir
solution.detail: The English East India Company established its first factory in India in 1613 during the reign of Mughal Emperor Jahangir (1605-1627). This historic milestone was achieved when Jahangir granted the Company permission to set up a trading post in Surat, following diplomatic efforts by Sir Thomas Roe. The factory in Surat marked the beginning of a sustained British commercial presence in India and was crucial for accessing the lucrative Indian spice and textile markets. Surat, being a major port city, was central to maritime trade in the Arabian Sea, attracting not only the British but also the Portuguese, Dutch, and other European powers.

stem: Who was the Viceroy of India when the Rowlatt Act was passed?

solution.detail: The Rowlatt Act was passed in 1919 during the viceroyalty of Lord Chelmsford (1916–1921). subject: Modern History
stem: Who among the following rejected the title of Knighthood and refused to accept a position in the Council of the Secretary of State for India?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Gopal Krishna Gokhale
solution.detail: Gopal Krishna Gokhale (1866–1915) was a moderate nationalist, social reformer, and one of the founding leaders of the Indian National Movement. He was a mentor to Mahatma Gandhi and played a key role in influencing early nationalist politics. The British government offered Gokhale the title of Knighthood and a position in the Council of the Secretary of State for India but he chose to decline both honors. His refusal was based on his commitment to India's self-governance, as he believed accepting such honors would mean complicity with colonial rule. He was a strong advocate of constitutional reforms and gradual political progress, promoting Swaraj (selfrule) within the British Empire.

subject: Modern History
stem: During the Indian Freedom Struggle, who of the following raised an army called ‘Free Indian Legion’?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Subhas Chandra Bose
solution.detail: During World War II, Subhas Chandra Bose (1897– 1945) raised an army called the Free Indian Legion (Azad Hind Legion) with the help of Nazi Germany. This was part of his efforts to liberate India from British rule by seeking foreign assistance. In 1941 he escaped from British house arrest in India and reached Germany via Afghanistan and the Soviet Union. In 1942, he formed the Free Indian Legion (also called the "Legion Freies Indien" in German) composed of Indian prisoners of war (POWs) captured by German forces in North Africa. This legion was trained by the Nazis and was intended to be deployed in British India through Afghanistan. However, as the war situation changed he shifted his base to Japan in 1943, where he took charge of the Indian National Army (INA), which had been established earlier by Rashbehari Bose.

subject: Modern History
stem: Who gave a systematic critique of the moderate politics of the Indian National Congress in a series of articles entitled New Lamps for Old?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Aurobindo Ghosh
solution.detail: Aurobindo Ghosh, a prominent figure in the Indian independence movement, provided a scathing critique of the moderate politics of the Indian National Congress in his series of articles titled New Lamps for Old. In these articles, he argued that the moderate approach of relying on petitions, prayers, and constitutional reforms was ineffective in achieving India’s independence. He advocated for a more aggressive and militant approach, including boycotts, strikes, and civil disobedience.

subject: Modern History
stem: Who among the following used the phrase ‘Un- British’ to criticize the English colonial control of India?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Dadabhai Naoroji
solution.detail: Naoroji, the ‘Grand Old Man of India,’ pioneered the analysis of British rule’s economic impact in his work Poverty and Un-British Rule in India (1901). His theory of economic drain exposed the exploitation of India’s resources by the British, fueling nationalist sentiment. By calling British rule “Un-British,” Naoroji critiqued the hypocrisy of colonial claims, challenging the narrative of British benevolence. His critique laid the foundation for future debates on colonial exploitation and inspired future leaders advocating for India’s self-rule.

subject: Modern History
stem: Who among the following wrote the poem, Subh-e Azadi?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Faiz Ahmed Faiz
solution.detail: Faiz Ahmed Faiz (1911–1984) was one of the most celebrated Urdu poets of the 20th century. He was a prominent figure in progressive literature and a vocal advocate for social justice, human rights, and anti-imperialism. The poem "Subh-e- Azadi" (Dawn of Freedom) was written by Faiz in 1947 which reflects on the partition of India and the bittersweet nature of independence. The poem captures the disillusionment and pain of partition, juxtaposing the joy of freedom with the tragedy of communal violence and displacement. The opening lines of the poem, "Ye daagh daagh ujala, ye shab-gazida sahar" (This stained light, this night-bitten dawn), are iconic and encapsulate the mixed emotions of the time.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Selene-1, the lunar orbiter mission belongs to which one of the following?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Japan
solution.detail: Selene-1 also known as Kaguya is a lunar orbiter mission launched by the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) using the H-IIA rocket. Its primary objectives were to study the Moon’s origin, evolution, and surface features with unprecedented detail. It mapped the Moon’s mineral composition and gravity anomalies which provided crucial data about its internal structure and geology. As one of the most advanced lunar exploration missions of its time contributing significantly to understanding the Moon’s evolution and mineral distribution.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: ISRO successfully conducted a rocket test using cryogenic engines in the year 2007. Where is the test stand used for the purpose, located?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Mahendragiri
solution.detail: ISRO successfully conducted a rocket test using cryogenic engines at the Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre (LPSC) located in Mahendragiri in Tamil Nadu. ISRO Propulsion Complex (IPRC) is equipped with the state-of-theart facilities necessary for realising the cutting edge propulsion technology products for the Indian space programme. Formerly, IPRC was known as LPSC, Mahendragiri. The IPRC is responsible for developing cryogenic propulsion systems essential for highperformance launch vehicles. A cryogenic engine uses supercooled liquids, typically liquid oxygen (LOX) and liquid hydrogen (LH2) as propellants. These engines are highly efficient because cryogenic fuels have a higher specific impulse, meaning they provide greater thrust for a given mass of fuel.

This advanced robotic spacecraft was designed to study Saturn, its rings, and its moons in extraordinary detail. As part of the mission, Cassini carried the ESA-built Huygens probe, which landed on Saturn’s largest moon, Titan. During its descent through Titan’s dense atmosphere, Huygens captured remarkable images and data, eventually landing on an icy floodplain damp with liquid methane.

solution.detail: India purchased the Barak anti-missile defence systems from Israel. The Barak system is developed by Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI) in collaboration with Rafael Advanced Defense Systems, is a surface-to-air missile designed to intercept and destroy incoming threats such as anti-ship missiles, aircraft, and drones. It is part of India’s naval defence system and is installed on various Indian Navy warships to enhance their anti-missile capabilities.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: In the context of the Indian defence, what is “Dhruv”?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Advanced light helicopter
solution.detail: 5.5 ton class helicopter with twin engines. is an Advanced Light Helicopter (ALH) developed by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) for military and civilian applications. Equipped with twin Shakti engines, it operates at altitudes up to 20,000 feet, making it suitable for diverse terrains. Dhruv is used for troop transport, search and rescue, reconnaissance, medical evacuation, and disaster relief. Its variants include the armed Rudra and the upgraded Mark III. As a symbol of India’s selfreliance in defense, Dhruv has been exported to countries like Nepal and Mauritius, enhancing India’s defense diplomacy.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: In which one of the following locations is the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) project to be built?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Southern France
solution.detail: ITER, which stands for International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor, is a massive international collaboration aimed at harnessing the power of nuclear fusion. This groundbreaking project is being constructed in Cadarache, Southern France. ITER aims to replicate nuclear fusion, where light atomic nuclei combine to release vast energy, offering a limitless, clean power source. Using a tokamak design, it employs powerful magnetic fields to heat plasma to over 150 million degrees Celsius. The project is a global collaboration involving China, the EU, India, Japan, Korea, Russia, and the US. India has provided in-wall shielding components and the cryo lines and cryo distribution system, crucial for the reactor’s operation.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following statements in respect of a jet engine and a rocket: 1. A jet engine uses the surrounding air for its oxygen supply and so is unsuitable for motion in space. 2. A rocket carries its own supply of oxygen in the gas form, and fuel. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: A jet engine relies on the oxygen in the Earth’s atmosphere for combustion. In space, where there is no atmosphere, a jet engine cannot function because it cannot obtain the necessary oxygen for burning fuel. Statement 2 is incorrect: Rockets do carry their own supply of oxygen, but typically, the oxygen is not in gas form. It is usually stored as a liquid oxidizer (e.g., liquid oxygen, LOX) along with the fuel.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What are Rubies and Sapphires chemically known as?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Aluminium oxide
solution.detail: Option (b) is correct: Rubies and sapphires are both varieties of the mineral corundum, which is chemically known as aluminium oxide (Al2O3). The difference between rubies and sapphires lies in the trace elements present in them. Rubies get their red color from the presence of trace amounts of chromium. Sapphires can come in various colors, but they are typically blue due to trace amounts of iron and titanium. Options (a), (c) and (d) are incorrect: Silicon dioxide (SiO2) is the chemical compound for quartz, Lead tetroxide (Pb4O6) is a compound containing lead and oxygen and Boron nitride (BN) is a compound of boron and nitrogen. All three are not related to rubies or sapphires.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following is also called Stranger Gas?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Xenon
solution.detail:
Option (c) is correct: Xenon: Approximately 0.0000087% of Earth’s atmosphere, known as “stranger gas” due to its unusual behavior among noble gases. Unlike other noble gases, xenon can form compounds with other elements, such as xenon fluorides. It is used in high-intensity discharge lamps and flash lamps for bright illumination, explored as an anesthetic agent and in imaging techniques for the brain, heart, and lungs, and utilized in ion propulsion systems for satellites, contributing to efficient space travel. Options (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect:
- Argon (Ar): Approximately 0.934% of Earth’s atmosphere, used in welding to create an inert atmosphere, in incandescent and fluorescent light bulbs to protect filaments from oxidation, and has potential as a neuroprotectant in medical applications.
- Neon: Neon (Ne) makes up about 0.0018% of Earth’s atmosphere. It is known for its use in neon signs that emit a reddish-orange glow. Neon is also used in lasers with helium to create specific light wavelengths. It helps detect leaks by identifying trace gases and is used as a cryogenic material for preserving biological specimens.
- Nitrous Oxide: Nitrous Oxide (N₂O), also called laughing gas, is colorless, odorless, and non-flammable. It has anesthetic properties and is used for minor surgeries and dental procedures. In the culinary field, it is used in whipped cream dispensers. In racing, it enhances engine power by allowing more fuel to burn, improving performance.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following pairs of metals constitutes the lightest metal and the heaviest metal, respectively?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Lithium and osmium
solution.detail:
- Lithium has a density of 0.534 g/cm3 and an atomic weight of 6.94 u, making it the lightest metal. Lithium has a large atomic radius compared to its atomic mass, leading to weak metallic bonding and low density.
- Osmium has the highest density of any naturally occurring metal, about 22.59 g/cm3, and an atomic weight of 190.23 u. Transition metals like osmium have small atomic radii and strong metallic bonds, leading to their high density and mass. Hence, Osmium is the heaviest metal. Options (a), (c)and (d) are incorrect: While lithium is the lightest, mercury (density 13.6 g/cm3) is not the heaviest metal. Also, Aluminium (density 2.7 g/cm3) is not the lightest metal.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What is Bisphenol A (BPA)?
options: A medical test for detecting cancer
A test for testing the use of drugs to improve performance by athletes
A chemical used for the development of food packaging materials
A special type of alloy steel

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: A chemical used for the development of food packaging materials
solution.detail: Bisphenol A (BPA) is an industrial chemical extensively used in the production of polycarbonate plastics and epoxy resins. Polycarbonate plastics made using BPA are commonly employed in the manufacture of reusable water bottles, food containers, and baby bottles due to their durability and lightweight properties. Epoxy resins containing BPA are used as coatings for the inside of food and beverage cans to prevent corrosion and contamination. However, BPA is a controversial substance because it can leach into food and beverages under high temperatures or prolonged storage. It is considered a potential endocrine disruptor that may interfere with hormonal activity, prompting regulatory bodies worldwide to evaluate its safety in food-related applications.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Mixture of which one of the following pairs of gases is the cause of occurrence of most of the explosions in mines?
options: Carbon dioxide and methane Science and Technology 607

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Methane and air
solution.detail: Methane and air are responsible for mine explosions, often referred to as fire damp explosions. Methane (CH₄) is a flammable gas that accumulates in coal mines. When mixed with air in the presence of a spark, it can cause deadly explosions. Options (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect: While Hydrogen and Oxygen are explosive, these gases are not significantly common in mining environments. Oxygen and Acetylene are used in welding, not relevant to mine explosions. Carbon Dioxide and Methane: Carbon dioxide is inert and cannot contribute to mine explosions.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following: 1. Rice fields 2. Coal mining 3. Domestic animals 4. Wet lands Which of the above are sources of methane, a major greenhouse gas?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2, 3 and 4
solution.detail: Methane (CH₄) is a potent greenhouse gas with a global warming potential over 25 times greater than carbon dioxide (CO₂) over a 100-year period. The primary sources of methane emissions can be categorized into natural and anthropogenic (human-caused) sources. Option 1 is correct: Rice fields emit methane due to the anaerobic decomposition of organic matter in waterlogged conditions. This is a significant contributor to global methane emissions, particularly in rice-producing countries. Option 2 is correct: Coal mining releases methane trapped within coal seams during the mining process. This is known as fugitive methane emissions and is a key source of industrial methane. Option 3 is correct: Domestic animals, particularly ruminants like cattle and sheep, produce methane during enteric fermentation, a digestive process where microbes break down food in the stomach. Option 4 is correct: Wetlands are natural sources of methane, produced through the anaerobic decomposition of organic material in water-saturated soils.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following is an insectivorous plant?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Pitcher plant
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: Passion flower plant is not an insectivorous plant. Passion flowers are flowering plants, typically known for their beautiful blooms and use in ornamental gardening. Option (b) is correct: Pitcher plant is an insectivorous plant that derives nutrients by trapping and digesting insects. Its leaves are modified into pitcher-like structures containing digestive fluids. Insects are lured by the plant’s bright colors and nectar and are trapped when they fall into the pitcher. This adaptation helps the plant survive in nutrient-poor soils such as those lacking nitrogen. Option (c) is incorrect: Night queen also known as Raat ki Rani, this is a type of flowering plant known for its fragrant flowers that bloom at night. It is not insectivorous. Option (d) is incorrect: Flame of the forest is a flowering tree (also known as Butea monosperma), primarily used for its ornamental and medicinal properties, and is not an insectivorous plant.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following statements about probiotic food: 1. Probiotic food contains live bacteria which are considered beneficial to humans. 2. Probiotic food helps in maintaining gut flora. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Probiotic foods are those that contain live microorganisms, typically bacteria or yeast, which confer health benefits to the host (usually humans) when consumed in adequate amounts. Common examples include sauerkraut, miso, tempeh, pickles, lassi, kombucha, and natto. Statement 1 is correct: Probiotic food contains live beneficial bacteria, such as Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium, which promote gut health. These bacteria are often present in fermented foods like yogurt, kimchi, and kefir. Statement 2 is correct: Probiotic food plays a key role in maintaining a healthy gut flora by balancing the microorganisms in the digestive system. This is crucial for digestion, immune function, and preventing gastrointestinal disorders such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

subject: Science and Tech
stem: In which of the following kinds of organisms is the phenomenon found wherein the female kills the male after copulation?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Spider
solution.detail: The intriguing phenomenon of sexual cannibalism, where the female kills the male after copulation, is observed in some species of spiders. In certain species like the Australian redback spider and the black widow, the female spider consumes the male after mating. Option (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect: Dragonflies do not exhibit sexual cannibalism in the same way spiders do. While females may sometimes be aggressive toward males, they do not typically kill them after copulation. In honeybees, the male (drone) dies after mating, but this is not due to the female killing him. After mating, the drone’s genitalia are ripped off during copulation, leading to death. Pit vipers, such as rattlesnakes, do not exhibit sexual cannibalism. After mating, the male and female go separate ways. There is no phenomenon where the female kills the male after copulation.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: For which one of the following snakes is the diet mainly composed of other snakes?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: King cobra
solution.detail: The king cobra (Ophiophagus hannah) is a snake whose diet primarily consists of other snakes, including venomous species such as kraits, pythons, and even other cobras. The name “Ophiophagus” literally means “snake-eater.” King Cobras are solitary creatures and are primarily active during the day (diurnal). King Cobras are found in forests, grasslands, and hilly terrains across South and Southeast Asia. The King Cobra is the longest venomous snake in the world, growing up to 5.6 meters (18.5 feet).

subject: Science and Tech
stem: What is the pH level of blood of a normal person?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 7.35–7.45
solution.detail: The pH level of blood in a normal person typically ranges from 7.35 to 7.45. This is considered slightly alkaline and is crucial for maintaining the proper function of enzymes and other biochemical processes in the body. The normal pH range of blood is tightly regulated by the body through mechanisms like buffer systems (such as the bicarbonate buffer system) to prevent drastic shifts in pH that could be harmful. A pH below 7.35 is considered acidosis, while a pH above 7.45 is considered alkalosis. Both conditions can disrupt normal physiological processes. The body maintains this narrow pH range to ensure optimal conditions for enzyme activity, oxygen transport, and metabolic reactions. 417. subject: Science and Tech
stem: Among the following, which one lays eggs and does not produce young ones directly?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Echidna
solution.detail: Option (a) is correct: The echidna is a monotreme, a group of egg-laying mammals that includes the platypus and echidna. Unlike most mammals, monotremes do not give birth to live young. They lay eggs and after laying eggs, the female echidna incubates them in a special pouch-like structure on her abdomen. Once the eggs hatch, the mother nurses her young by secreting milk from mammary glands, though monotremes do not have nipples. The milk oozes through the skin and the young lap it up.They are found in Australia and New Guinea and are adapted to a variety of environments, including forests and deserts. Options (b), (c) and (d) are incorrect: Kangaroos are marsupials, meaning they give birth to live young that are underdeveloped at birth. The baby, called a joey, continues to develop in the mother’s pouch. Porcupines are placental mammals and they give birth to live young. These mammals develop their young inside the mother’s body before birth. Whales are placental mammals and give birth to live young after a long gestation period.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: The release of which one of the following into ponds and wells helps in controlling the mosquitoes?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Gambusia fish
solution.detail: The Gambusia fish (Gambusia affinis) or mosquito fish is highly effective in controlling mosquito populations in stagnant water such as ponds and wells. These fish feed on mosquito larvae, reducing the number of adult mosquitoes. As biological control agents, Gambusia fish offer a natural and chemical-free method for controlling mosquitoes. They are particularly useful in limiting the spread of diseases like malaria, dengue, and Zika. By consuming mosquito larvae in standing water, they help decrease the overall mosquito population, supporting public health efforts.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Among the following, which one is not an ape?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Langur
solution.detail: Primates are a diverse group of mammals divided into two major categories: monkeys and apes. While monkeys and apes share common ancestors, they differ significantly in their characteristics, behavior, and evolutionary history. Option (c) is correct: Langurs are Old World monkeys(and not ape) and belong to the family Cercopithecidae. Unlike apes, langurs have tails, a key feature that distinguishes them from apes. They are primarily found in South and Southeast Asia. These monkeys are known for their long limbs and often live in large social groups. Langurs feed on leaves, fruits, and seeds, and are often seen foraging in groups. Their tails help them maintain balance while moving through the trees. They are also much smaller in size than the great apes like gorillas. Options (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect: Gibbons are lesser apes, belonging to the family Hylobatidae. They are known for their remarkable brachiation (swinging from tree to tree using their long arms), making them one of the most agile primates. Gibbons are found in Southeast Asia and are relatively small compared to other apes. Gorillas are great apes, the largest of all living primates belonging to the family Hominidae. Gorillas are native to Central Africa and live in groups led by a dominant male known as a silverback. Gorillas are primarily terrestrial, spending most of their time on the ground, unlike the gibbon, which is arboreal. Gorillas, like all apes, do not have tails, and they share a close genetic relationship with humans. Orangutans are great apes of the genus Pongo. They are highly intelligent and spend most of their time in trees, primarily in the rainforests of Borneo and Sumatra. Orangutans have remarkable cognitive abilities, using tools and even creating nests to sleep in.
